channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Before running for Congress, Bobby Pulido was a Tejano music icon
item.description: Pulido has been a mainstay of Tejano music —a genre blending traditional regional Mexican elements with country, pop and conjunto influences — for more than three decades.
item.content:encoded:
Pulido has been a mainstay of Tejano music —a genre blending traditional regional Mexican elements with country, pop and conjunto influences — for more than three decades.

(Image credit: Bloomberg/Bloomberg via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Isabella Gomez Sarmiento

item.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.
item.content:encoded:
The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran fires more missiles at Israel and rejects Trump's talk as 'fake news' for markets
item.description: Israeli health officials said Iranian missiles struck four sites across Israel Tuesday, including central Tel Aviv, injuring at least six people. Iranian authorities also said a gas supply line in southwest Iran was struck overnight.
item.content:encoded:
Israeli health officials said Iranian missiles struck four sites across Israel Tuesday, including central Tel Aviv, injuring at least six people. Iranian authorities also said a gas supply line in southwest Iran was struck overnight.

(Image credit: Jalaa Marey)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
item.description: In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.
item.content:encoded:
In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.

(Image credit: 朝鮮通信社/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
item.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.
item.content:encoded:
Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

(Image credit: Andy Wong/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
item.description: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.
item.content:encoded:
The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

(Image credit: U.S. Navy)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Denmark holds early elections spurred by Trump's threats to take Greenland
item.description: Denmark's prime minister called early parliamentary elections after gaining a popularity boost from standing up to President Trump over his threat to seize Greenland.
item.content:encoded:
Denmark's prime minister called early parliamentary elections after gaining a popularity boost from standing up to President Trump over his threat to seize Greenland.

(Image credit: Rob Schmitz)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: As parents age, their children face hard choices about when to take the car keys
item.description: States have many policies to stop risky older drivers from renewing their licenses. But in practice, it's often adult children who must decide when to take the car keys away from an aging parent.
item.content:encoded:
States have many policies to stop risky older drivers from renewing their licenses. But in practice, it's often adult children who must decide when to take the car keys away from an aging parent.

(Image credit: Joel Rose)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Trump takes aim at windmills despite increasing energy costs
item.description: President Trump's mission to fight renewable wind energy comes at a time of rising energy costs.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's mission to fight renewable wind energy comes at a time of rising energy costs.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: ICE deployments created chaos for cities and cost them millions, NPR analysis finds
item.description: Local leaders report already-strapped police departments racked up overtime bills in the millions while others report a multi-million dollar hit to business during the worst ICE surges.
item.content:encoded: Local leaders report already-strapped police departments racked up overtime bills in the millions while others report a multi-million dollar hit to business during the worst ICE surges.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz
